content:
- Marked story-11 task 04 done, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed all implementation plus Ralph bookkeeping as `b49d50e`, and pushed `master`.
- Validation that shipped with the commit: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed.
- If another context picks up here, this task is complete and the worktree should be clean.

- Read the previous codex progress entry, refreshed Ralph summaries, inspected active task files plus top-level Cargo/Makefile and runner E2E support modules to determine the actual stack and current architecture.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, no visible blocker tags on the active failing tasks, and no higher-priority/meta tasks that outrank the failing-task branch. The repo is a Rust workspace with the main implementation in `crates/runner`, existing E2E harness support, and completed story-11 chaos tasks 01-04 already in done.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/05-task-e2e-source-high-write-churn-during-transfer.md` as the next task and wrote it to `.ralph/current_task.txt`. Next context should start that task with the `tdd` skill and validate with `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` plus `tdd` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is story-11 task 05: `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/05-task-e2e-source-high-write-churn-during-transfer.md`.
- Next I am reading the task file and story context to decide whether to create a plan file with `TO BE VERIFIED` / `NOW EXECUTE` or proceed with implementation.

- Read the active task file. It is still pre-execution (`<status>not_started</status>`, `<passes>false</passes>`), so the required next step is to create a dedicated plan file and stop after the planning handoff.
- The task goal is a real E2E test for heavy source-side create/delete churn during migration, with an explicit decision record about whether current design remains stable.
- Next I am comparing prior story-11 plan files and existing E2E/chaos test code so the new plan matches established repo structure and can explicitly call out a boundary cleanup opportunity.

- Inspected the current long-lane harness and support modules. The workspace already contains destination write-failure support and two new long-lane transaction-failure scenarios matching task 05's acceptance focus.
- `git status --short` shows only Ralph bookkeeping files are currently dirty/untracked; there are no code-file modifications to preserve in this workspace right now.
- Next I am checking how prior tasks recorded plan paths so I can add the missing task-05 planning artifact and hand off cleanly for the execution turn.

- Confirmed task 05 has no plan file yet; prior story-11 tasks record a `<plan>...</plan>` tag directly in the task markdown.
- Read the design docs and current long-lane support. Existing coverage handles single-update retries, deletes, crash/restart, and transaction failures, but there is no dedicated high source create/update/delete churn scenario yet.
- Boundary focus for the plan: avoid stuffing churn SQL and expected-state logic into `default_bootstrap_long_lane.rs`; instead add typed churn helpers under the default bootstrap harness while keeping generic polling/tracking in `e2e_harness.rs`.

- Created the missing task-05 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/05-task-e2e-source-high-write-churn-during-transfer_plans/2026-04-19-source-write-churn-e2e-plan.md` and linked it from the task markdown.
- The plan is already promoted to `NOW EXECUTE`. It uses `tdd` slices and an `improve-code-boundaries` focus that keeps churn orchestration in `default_bootstrap_harness.rs` instead of bloating `default_bootstrap_long_lane.rs`.
- Next context should read the new plan, execute it exactly, and only mark the task done after `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all pass plus the task file records the churn verdict.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and both required skill instructions from disk.
- Confirmed the active task is story-11 task 05 and prior context already created a plan file promoted to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task markdown and plan contents, then I will execute the plan with TDD unless the design looks wrong enough to revert the plan to TO BE VERIFIED.

- Read the task markdown and execution plan. The plan is already in NOW EXECUTE, so I am proceeding with the TDD slices.
- Next I am inspecting the existing long-lane test and support harnesses to identify the public helpers I can reuse and the narrow customer-specific boundary to add for source churn.

- Inspected the current long-lane and harness code. Most generic pieces already exist: destination polling, helper-table polling, tracking progress reads, verify, and runner liveness checks.
- Next I am locating the exact helper methods and adding the first failing long-lane slice with the smallest customer-specific support for deterministic source churn.

- Added the first RED slice: a new ignored long-lane test for high source customer write churn during transfer.
- Running that single ignored test now in its failing state before adding the typed customer-specific churn helper boundary.

- Added `CustomerWriteChurnExpectation` plus a customer-specific high-churn source workload helper in `default_bootstrap_harness.rs`.
- Re-running the single ignored long-lane test now to see whether the tracer-bullet slice passes or exposes the first real runtime gap.

- The tracer-bullet long-lane slice passed after adding a typed customer-specific churn helper boundary.
- Next I am strengthening the same scenario with helper-shadow and tracking-progress assertions, and I am checking the improve-code-boundaries skill directory for any extra smell guidance before the final boundary review.

- The high-churn long-lane test now passes with helper-shadow, tracking, liveness, stability, and verify assertions.
- Next I am reviewing the current diff against the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance, then I will run the required full repository lanes: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long.

- Full validation started. `make check` and `make lint` both passed.
- `make test` is running now; after that I still need `make test-long`, then task/bookkeeping updates, commit, and push if every lane stays green.

- All required validation passed: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long.
- Next I am updating the task/plan bookkeeping with the churn verdict, then I will run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all changes including Ralph state, and push.

- Updated the task markdown to done with an explicit verdict that the current design handled bounded churn acceptably, and checked off the execution plan.
- Running `.ralph/task_switch.sh` now; next I will stage all changes, commit with validation evidence, and push.
